# Risk It All: Cozy Couple Room
> Browser-first cozy couple-room game foundation built with React, Vite, React Three Fiber, and Firebase-ready skeletons. The active runtime today is a local single-room sandbox, not the older multiplayer backend path.

## Canonical Docs
- [README](README.md): project overview, setup, and current runtime summary.
- [AI handoff](docs/AI_HANDOFF.md): current runtime truth, implemented systems, and regression guardrails.
- [Codebase map](docs/CODEBASE_MAP.md): fastest folder-by-folder navigation after the refactor.
- [Current systems](docs/CURRENT_SYSTEMS.md): gameplay and runtime behavior that exists now.
- [Architecture](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): file ownership, boundaries, and system responsibilities.
- [MCP setup](docs/MCP_SETUP.md): recommended MCP stack for this repo.

## Active Runtime Entry Points
- [src/App.tsx](src/App.tsx): top-level sandbox orchestrator.
- [src/components/RoomView.tsx](src/components/RoomView.tsx): live 3D scene/runtime controller.
- [src/app](src/app): app-shell components and hooks extracted from App.
- [src/components/room-view](src/components/room-view): scene-support modules extracted from RoomView.
- [src/lib/roomState.ts](src/lib/roomState.ts): active room schema and starter layout.
- [src/lib/furnitureRegistry.ts](src/lib/furnitureRegistry.ts): canonical furniture catalog.
- [src/lib/devLocalState.ts](src/lib/devLocalState.ts): local persistence and migration.
- [src/lib/economy.ts](src/lib/economy.ts): buy and sell rules.
- [src/lib/pcMinigame.ts](src/lib/pcMinigame.ts): desk PC minigame rules and rewards.
- [src/lib/worldLighting.ts](src/lib/worldLighting.ts): world-clock lighting calculations.

## Navigation Heuristics
- For toolbar, inventory, and app-shell UI changes, start in `src/app`.
- For 3D scene, interaction, camera, and placement behavior, start in `src/components/RoomView.tsx` and then inspect `src/components/room-view`.
- For rules, persistence, room schema, economy, or furniture metadata, start in `src/lib`.
- For new furniture, update the registry first, then rendering, then tests.

## Important Invariants
- Preserve the split between `ownedFurniture` and placed `furniture`.
- Keep registry-driven metadata as the source of truth for furniture behavior.
- Preserve `anchorFurnitureId` and `surfaceLocalOffset` for surface decor.
- Do not replace the active sandbox schema in `roomState.ts` with the older backend-oriented `types.ts` shape.
- Starter furniture can be removed, but it must not mint sell coins.
- Window furniture must keep wall-opening behavior.

## Tech Stack
- React 18.3
- Vite 6
- TypeScript 5.7
- three 0.173
- @react-three/fiber 8.17
- @react-three/drei 9.122
- @react-three/postprocessing 2.19
- postprocessing 6.38
- leva 0.10
- Vitest 3

## Commands
- `cmd /c npm install`
- `cmd /c npm run dev`
- `cmd /c npm test`
- `cmd /c npm run build`

## Test Entry Points
- [tests/economy.test.ts](tests/economy.test.ts)
- [tests/furnitureCollision.test.ts](tests/furnitureCollision.test.ts)
- [tests/furnitureInteractions.test.ts](tests/furnitureInteractions.test.ts)
- [tests/furnitureRegistry.test.ts](tests/furnitureRegistry.test.ts)
- [tests/pcMinigame.test.ts](tests/pcMinigame.test.ts)
- [tests/roomState.test.ts](tests/roomState.test.ts)
- [tests/surfaceDecor.test.ts](tests/surfaceDecor.test.ts)
- [tests/wallOpenings.test.ts](tests/wallOpenings.test.ts)
- [tests/worldLighting.test.ts](tests/worldLighting.test.ts)

## Legacy Boundary
The active runtime is the local sandbox path centered on `App.tsx`, `RoomView.tsx`, `furnitureRegistry.ts`, `roomState.ts`, and `devLocalState.ts`. Older Firebase/auth/pairing and couple-room files still exist, but they are not the current runtime entry path.
